person.name: Joan Empson
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary: Joan Stapleton, first wife of Sir William Pierrepont of Holme Pierrepont, co. Notts. She's the mother of Elizabeth Pierrepont, wife of John Sacheverell.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Empson於大約1466年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特時，她的父親Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy17歲，母親Jane de Plumpton22歲。她於1502年4月7日與Henry Sothill結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1510年7月16日之後在英國，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Empson于大约1466年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特时，她的父亲Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy17岁，母亲Jane de Plumpton22岁。她于1502年4月7日与Henry Sothill结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1510年7月16日之后在英国，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Empson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: metronymic meaning ‘son of Emma’, from the Middle English female personal name Em(e), Emme (see Imm ) + -son.
shortened form of Emerson . The two names were no doubt confused, and it is possible that the reverse process sometimes occurred, whereby Em(m)erson was an altered form of Emmeson, i.e. Em(p)son.

variants.name: Imeson
meanings: English: variant either of Emmeson (see Empson ) or of Emerson .

variants.name: Impson
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Empson  . This form of the surname is now more common in the US than in Britain.

variants.name: Imm
meanings: German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Im(m)o, a pet form of names based on Irmin-, a divine or ethnic name formed with irmin, ermen ‘whole, entire’.

English (Wiltshire): from the ancient Germanic female personal name Imma, Emma, of the same origin as in 1 above. In Old English Imma, Emma was borne by both males and females. In Middle English, under Norman influence, the personal name Emm(a), Imm(a) came to be used almost exclusively for women, being taken as a short form of Ermingard.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Empson was born about 1466, in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy, was 17 and her mother, Jane de Plumpton, was 22. She married Henry Sothill on 7 April 1502. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died after 16 July 1510, in England, and was buried in London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Empson est née environ 1466, à Towcester, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Jane de Plumpton, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Henry Sothill le 7 avril 1502. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée après 16 juillet 1510, à Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Empson um 1466 in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy 17 und ihre Mutter Jane de Plumpton 22. Sie hat am 7. April 1502 Henry Sothill geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb nach 16. Juli 1510 in England und wurde in London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Empson è nata il circa 1466 nel Towcester, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy, aveva 17 anni e sua madre, Jane de Plumpton, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Henry Sothill il 7 aprile 1502. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì dopo il 16 luglio 1510, nel England, e fu sepolta a London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Empsonが約1466年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアトウセスターで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchyは17歳，母Jane de Plumptonは22歳であった。1502年4月7日にHenry Sothillと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1510年7月16日以後にイギリスで死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Empson(이)가 대략 1466년 영국 노르섬프토실르 타우스스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane de Plumpton(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1502년 4월 7일 Henry Sothill(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1510년 7월 16일 이후 영국에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Empson nasceu aproximadamente 1466, em Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Jane de Plumpton, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry Sothill em 7 de abril de 1502. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu após 16 de julho de 1510, em Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Empson родилась около 1466 г. в местности тоусестир, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane de Plumpton, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Sothill 7 апреля 1502 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла после 16 июля 1510 г. в местности Англия и была похоронена в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Empson nació aproximadamente 1466, en Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Jane de Plumpton, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Henry Sothill el 7 de abril de 1502. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió después de 16 de julio de 1510, en Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
